req.title: Session management
req.statement: A user session shall expire after 30 minutes of inactivity; expired sessions shall redirect to login.

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for user session expiration requirement:
"A user session shall expire after 30 minutes of inactivity;
expired sessions shall redirect to login."
"""

import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures & helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

SESSION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 1800  # 30 minutes


@pytest.fixture
def session_store():
    """In-memory session store used by the application under test."""
    from app.session import SessionStore  # adjust import to your project layout

    return SessionStore()


@pytest.fixture
def authenticated_session(session_store):
    """Return a freshly created, authenticated session token."""
    token = session_store.create_session(user_id="user-42")
    return token


@pytest.fixture
def client(app):
    """Flask / Django / FastAPI test client – adapt to your framework."""
    return app.test_client()


@pytest.fixture
def logged_in_client(client):
    """Test client that has already completed login."""
    client.post("/login", data={"username": "testuser", "password": "correct-pw"})
    return client


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Session metadata – timeout value
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestSessionTimeoutConfiguration:
    def test_session_timeout_is_30_minutes(self, session_store):
        """The configured timeout must equal exactly 30 minutes."""
        assert session_store.timeout_seconds == SESSION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS

    def test_session_stores_last_activity_timestamp(self, session_store, authenticated_session):
        """A new session must record an initial last-activity timestamp."""
        session = session_store.get_session(authenticated_session)
        assert session.last_activity is not None
        assert isinstance(session.last_activity, datetime)

    def test_new_session_is_not_immediately_expired(self, session_store, authenticated_session):
        """A brand-new session must not be expired."""
        assert not session_store.is_expired(authenticated_session)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Activity tracking – last-activity timestamp updates
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestActivityTracking:
    def test_activity_updates_last_activity_timestamp(self, session_store, authenticated_session):
        """Any recorded activity must refresh the last-activity timestamp."""
        session_before = session_store.get_session(authenticated_session)
        original_ts = session_before.last_activity

        # Simulate a small passage of time then record activity
        with patch("app.session.datetime") as mock_dt:
            later = original_ts + timedelta(seconds=5)
            mock_dt.utcnow.return_value = later
            session_store.record_activity(authenticated_session)

        session_after = session_store.get_session(authenticated_session)
        assert session_after.last_activity > original_ts

    def test_activity_resets_expiry_window(self, session_store, authenticated_session):
        """Recording activity on a nearly-expired session must prevent expiry."""
        with patch("app.session.datetime") as mock_dt:
            # Jump to 29 minutes 50 seconds – still within window
            nearly_expired = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(seconds=1790)
            mock_dt.utcnow.return_value = nearly_expired
            session_store.record_activity(authenticated_session)

        # Now check at 30 minutes from *original* creation – should NOT be expired
        # because activity was recorded at 29 m 50 s
        with patch("app.session.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.utcnow.return_value = datetime.utcnow() + timedelt
